no-restricted-syntax.message: Do not cast to SupabaseClient — use the typed client from lib/supabase-admin.ts instead.

P2.4 rule 1: raw .insert/.update/.upsert on protected tables (service_deliveries, payments, accounts, contacts, tasks) must go through lib/operations/. Move this write through the appropriate helper (lib/operations/service-delivery.ts / payment.ts / portal.ts).

P2.4 rule 5: direct .rpc('exec_sql', ...) is only allowed in lib/mcp/tools/sql.ts (the execute_sql tool) and app/api/cron/audit-health-check/route.ts (the audit cron). Elsewhere, use dedicated MCP tools or crm_update_record.

P2.4 rule 6: the exec_sql RPC parameter is `sql_query`, not `query`. This bug silently caused the 2026-04-09→14 Phase 0 audit-cron zero-findings failure (plan §4 line 47). Rename the key to `sql_query`.

Phase D1: direct writes to `contacts.portal_tier` must go through `reconcileTier()` in lib/operations/portal.ts so contacts + linked accounts + auth.users.app_metadata all land on the same tier atomically. This rule exists because tier drift across the three layers was the root of the Luca Gallacci 2026-04-18 incident. Exempted inside lib/operations/**.
Do not cast to SupabaseClient — use the typed client from lib/supabase-admin.ts instead.
P2.4 rule 6: the exec_sql RPC parameter is `sql_query`, not `query`.
Do not cast to SupabaseClient — use the typed client from lib/supabase-admin.ts instead.

P2.4 rule 1: raw .insert/.update/.upsert on protected tables must go through lib/operations/.
P2.4 rule 5: direct .rpc('exec_sql', ...) is only allowed in lib/mcp/tools/sql.ts and app/api/cron/audit-health-check/route.ts.
P2.4 rule 6: the exec_sql RPC parameter is `sql_query`, not `query`.

P2.4 rule 3: form-completed handlers must destructure `error` from Supabase calls. Use `const { data, error } = await supabase.from(X).update(Y)` instead of `const result = await ...`. Silent write failures in form handlers are the exact mode that caused the itin-form-completed bug (plan line 73). If this is not a Supabase call, ignore this warning with a targeted eslint-disable-next-line.
